description:
You are assisting a web-developer who just checked a containerized build-agent into the company CI system.  
The developer noticed that the agent comes up with the wrong time-zone and locale, which in turn makes the web-framework timestamp formatting tests fail.  
Your job is to:

1. Configure the user-level shell environment so that every new interactive Bash session uses  
   • the time-zone “Europe/Paris” and  
   • the UTF-8 locale “fr_FR.UTF-8”.

2. Produce a short verification log so that the CI pipeline can automatically confirm the fix.

Constraints & precise success criteria
--------------------------------------
A. *Where to make the configuration*  
   • Edit the file /home/user/.bashrc (do **not** touch /etc files – you do not have root).  
   • Add two export lines **exactly** in the form  
     `export TZ="Europe/Paris"`  
     `export LC_ALL="fr_FR.UTF-8"`

B. *What the verification log must look like*  
   • Create the directory /home/user/dev_setup if it does not yet exist.  
   • Inside that directory create a file named time_locale.log whose content is **exactly** two lines (no extra blank lines, no leading/trailing spaces):  
```
TZ=Europe/Paris
LC_ALL=fr_FR.UTF-8
```
   • The file must end with a single UNIX newline after the second line.

C. *Command budget*  
   • You should be able to achieve everything with a simple sequence of three to four shell commands.

The automated test will check only the following:

1. /home/user/.bashrc contains the two export lines **verbatim**.  
2. /home/user/dev_setup/time_locale.log exists and its content matches the two-line format shown above, including the final newline.

Once you are done, print nothing but your normal shell prompts and command outputs; there is no extra narrative required.
truth:
Expected filesystem state after the agent completes:

1. /home/user/.bashrc  
   • Contains a line `export TZ="Europe/Paris"`  
   • Contains a line `export LC_ALL="fr_FR.UTF-8"`

(These can appear anywhere in the file. They must be present exactly once, spelling and quoting must match.)

2. /home/user/dev_setup/time_locale.log  
   • Plain-text file, permissions 0644 (default is fine).  
   • Content (including final newline):
TZ=Europe/Paris\n
LC_ALL=fr_FR.UTF-8\n

No additional lines or whitespace are allowed.

The tests will fail if either line is missing, misspelled, quoted differently, or if time_locale.log has extra/blank lines.
